Heritage World Coin Auctions - NYINC Signature Sale 3129, Lot 33018 — Ancients
MYSIA. Lampsacus. Ca. 395-350 BC. AV stater (18mm, 8.44 gm, 3h). NGC MS★ 5/5 - 4/5, Fine Style. Head of the hunter Actaeon left, wearing a stag's horn above his forehead / Forepart of Pegasus
sales.description:
Ancients
MYSIA. Lampsacus. Ca. 395-350 BC. AV stater (18mm, 8.44 gm, 3h). NGC MS★ 5/5 - 4/5, Fine Style. Head of the hunter Actaeon left, wearing a stag's horn above his forehead / Forepart of Pegasus right. Baldwin, 'The Gold Staters of Lampsakos', AJN 53, 1924, 33b-c, pl. III, 15-16. Gulbenkian 694. Jameson 1434. SNG France 5, 1145. Exquisite detail capturing the transformative scene. A stunning example of Mysian artistry.

From The Cambridge Collection. Ex Nomos, Auction 7 (15 May 2013), lo...

Ancients
ASIA MINOR. Uncertain. Ca. 5th century BC. AR tetartemorion (5mm, 0.11 gm). Fine. Three crescents and two pellets / Head of roaring lion left within square incuse. SNG Keckman -. SNG Rosen -. SNG Kayhan -. Cf. Nomos, Auction 23 (30 November 2021), 115; Savoca, Special Auction 79 (21 June 2020), lot 114; Numismatik Naumann, Auction 44 (7 June 2016), lot 190. Apparently unpublished in the standard references.
